messages.content: I want to do a cognitive athlete reframe. My relationship to fitness has to change. What are the actual goals?

Through questions: what would make you choose to train consistently when motivation isn't there? What has killed training before?

The answers you gave: kids are the primary driver — Cooper at 11 months, baby number 2 due October 2026. You want to keep up for 20+ years and set a physical example. The second driver is cognition — exercise is the single most evidence-backed intervention for brain health, and once you connected 'fitness is for my brain' to 'interest-based activation,' the engagement changed from should to will.

What's killed training before: depression plus exhaustion from carrying everything at home. Tough pregnancy and recovery, MBA, declining drinking problem, stealing 1-2am for sanity. That's capacity failure, not motivation failure. The system has to be antifragile to life disruption because life is going to disrupt it.

Principles you confirmed: cognition first, generalist, utility over aesthetics, minimum effective dose, first principles science only.

Target benchmarks: deadlift 2× bodyweight, squat 1.5×, bench 1.25×, 20 pullups, sub-21 3-mile, 12-mile ruck with 45 pounds, touch toes, floor-to-standing no hands. Target body composition 14-16% body fat at 210-225 pounds.

Timeline: prehab now through May, foundation May through October baby, resilience phase after October, build phase when stable.
What about the medication picture — that has to factor in.

Current stack: Adderall XR 20mg daily, IR 5mg as needed for MBA, Bupropion XL 150mg daily. Qelbree 200mg ER prescribed but not started.

Key diagnostic I want to name explicitly: you're running on stimulants masking chronic sleep deprivation. 4-6 hours of chemically-impaired sleep nightly. The burnout you've been attributing to capacity is substantially a sleep deficit. Stimulants are compensating.

For the AuDHD architecture, I identified guanfacine ER (Intuniv) as the missing piece. Alpha-2 agonist that modulates the ASD amplification without reducing ADHD treatment benefit. Published evidence specifically for ASD plus ADHD combination. This is the adjunct that addresses the ASD side of the equation.

Proposed 4-phase plan for your psychiatrist:
1. Try Qelbree first (non-stimulant, zero ASD amplification risk)
2. Add guanfacine ER regardless of what the primary medication becomes
3. If stimulant remains necessary, try methylphenidate class before escalating amphetamine dose
4. If amphetamine stays, Vyvanse or Dexedrine over Adderall combined with guanfacine

Sleep protocol replacing THC plus unisom: both current aids are actively harmful. THC suppresses REM. Unisom is anticholinergic with dementia risk on long-term use. Evening stack: magnesium glycinate 400mg, L-theanine 200mg, glycine 3g, then melatonin at 0.3mg (timing signal, not sedative).
I'm done with alcohol for now.

Noted and documented. Stated February 11, 2026. Framed as the single biggest antagonist to the cognitive goals — it directly undermines the sleep protocol, blunts BDNF response to exercise, and works against the supplement stack. The history is that alcohol was introduced as a sleep aid around 6th grade and escalated through your 20s. 'For now' is how you said it. I'm not adding weight to that qualifier.

facts: Cody's primary fitness motivation is his children — Cooper at 11 months and baby number 2 due October 2026
Exercise is identified as the single most evidence-backed intervention for brain health
Previous training was killed by depression, exhaustion from carrying everything at home, a tough pregnancy and recovery, MBA demands, and a declining drinking problem
Fitness targets are: deadlift 2x bodyweight, squat 1.5x, bench 1.25x, 20 pullups, sub-21 3-mile, 12-mile ruck with 45 pounds
Target body composition is 14-16% body fat at 210-225 pounds
Current medications: Adderall XR 20mg daily, Adderall IR 5mg as needed, Bupropion XL 150mg daily, Qelbree 200mg ER prescribed but not started
Cody is running on stimulants masking chronic sleep deprivation of 4-6 hours nightly with chemically-impaired sleep
Guanfacine ER (Intuniv) was identified as the missing piece for AuDHD — modulates ASD amplification without reducing ADHD treatment benefit
THC suppresses REM sleep and unisom is anticholinergic with dementia risk on long-term use
Cody stated he is done with alcohol as of February 11, 2026
Alcohol was introduced as a sleep aid around 6th grade and escalated through his 20s
decisions: Adopt the cognitive athlete frame — training primarily for brain health, not aesthetics
Build a training system antifragile to life disruption rather than relying on motivation or habits
Stop THC and unisom and replace with evidence-based evening supplement stack

metadata.notes: Sourced from syn/memory/2026-02-11.md — cognitive athlete framework and medication architecture for Syl session context
